name: Jormungandr's Scales
description: Jormungandr's Scales are a collection of shimmering, iridescent scales shed by the immense sea serpent Jormungandr, also known as the Midgard Serpent. Born of Loki and the giantess Angrboða, Jormungandr was cast into the ocean surrounding Midgard by Odin, where it grew to encircle the entire world, biting its own tail. These scales, imbued with the serpent's immense power and the primordial essence of the sea, are said to possess potent protective and destructive capabilities. They grant the wielder resistance to poisons, control over the tides, and a fragment of Jormungandr's formidable strength. Their shimmering surface reflects the endless depths of the ocean and holds whispers of forgotten secrets. Some believe the scales can be used to summon storms or even influence the serpent itself, though such attempts are fraught with peril. The scales are incredibly rare and scattered across the nine realms, often found near bodies of water or hidden within ancient ruins. Possessing even a single scale is considered a great boon, but also a grave responsibility, as the power they hold is immense and unpredictable. The scales resonate with the serpent's life force, and overuse can attract its attention, potentially leading to catastrophic consequences. Their true potential remains largely unknown, making them objects of both fascination and fear among those who know of their existence. Legends say that during Ragnarok, the scales will be instrumental in the serpent's final battle against Thor, influencing the outcome of the world-ending conflict. The scales are a potent symbol of the raw, untamed power of the natural world and the destructive potential that lies dormant beneath the surface.
subtitle: Scales of the World Serpent
origin: Shed from Jormungandr's body as it dwells in the ocean.
currentLocation: Scattered across the Nine Realms, primarily near oceans and ancient ruins.

keyMyths.title: Jormungandr's Birth
keyMyths.description: Jormungandr, born to Loki and Angrboða, was cast into the sea by Odin. There, it grew to encircle Midgard, becoming a symbol of the ocean's vastness and power.

keyMyths.title: Ragnarok's Tide
keyMyths.description: During Ragnarok, Jormungandr will rise from the sea, poisoning the land and battling Thor. The scales are believed to influence the serpent's actions in this final conflict.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: Provides foundational knowledge about Jormungandr's origin and role in Norse cosmology.
sources.text: Provides foundational knowledge about Jormungandr's origin and role in Norse cosmology.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: Collection of Old Norse poems that contain myths and legends, including references to Jormungandr.
sources.text: Collection of Old Norse poems that contain myths and legends, including references to Jormungandr.
